description:
## Goal

The Crystal GC runs minor collections automatically, but full
collections (which defragment the heap) only happen rarely. In
the 22h uptime we observed, the heap grew to 80 GB VIRT with
4.3 GB committed — full GC may not have run in a long time.

`GCCollector.maybe_collect` (src/fetcher/gc_collector.cr) does
trigger a GC every 5 minutes, but only if called. The
supervisor calls it after each refresh cycle, so if the
refresh interval is 30 min, GC runs every 30 min. That's
fine for normal operation but may not be enough if a memory
leak is happening between cycles.

## Design

Add a periodic full GC trigger in `MemoryManagerActor`:
- Every 30 min, run `GC.collect` twice (Crystal's recommended
  pattern for full collection)
- Log the heap size before/after so we can see the effect
- Don't force a GC on the hot path; only via a timer fiber

Or, more conservative: just lower the threshold in
`GCCollector` from 5 min to 1 min so it runs more often. The
supervisor already calls `maybe_collect` after each cycle, so
the new timer just provides a backstop.

## Why this might help

If the leak is "objects are kept alive", forcing GC won't help.
If the leak is "GC isn't running often enough to keep up",
forcing GC will reveal the rate of allocation. Either way,
it gives us data.

## Acceptance

- A timer triggers a forced GC at a configurable interval
- The reporter logs the heap size before/after each forced GC
- Build + spec + ameba clean
title: Add periodic forced full GC to MemoryManagerActor
